restatement: A tool that takes LaTeX source as input and renders it as a visible mathematical equation. The user types/pastes LaTeX, the tool shows the typeset math.

assumption_ledger.assumption: Input is LaTeX math syntax (the user supplies the source string).
assumption_ledger.rationale: Stated explicitly in the prompt: 'from LaTeX input'.
assumption_ledger.what_breaks_if_wrong: Wrong input format would invalidate the entire parsing/rendering pipeline.

assumption_ledger.assumption: Output is a visible rendered equation (the user sees typeset math, not just a transformed string).
assumption_ledger.rationale: The verb 'render' implies visual output, not transformation to another markup.
assumption_ledger.what_breaks_if_wrong: If output is meant to be a non-visual artifact (e.g., MathML string only), the UI surface would be wrong.

assumption_ledger.rationale: Prompt is silent on users/auth; default-not-present per heuristics. Equation rendering is a stateless utility.
assumption_ledger.what_breaks_if_wrong: If multi-user sharing was intended, would need backend + identity layer.

assumption_ledger.assumption: No persistence required; equations are rendered transiently in-session.
assumption_ledger.what_breaks_if_wrong: If user expected a saved equation library, they'd lose work between sessions.

assumption_ledger.assumption: Desktop / standard browser viewport is the primary target.
assumption_ledger.rationale: LaTeX authoring is overwhelmingly a keyboard-heavy, desktop activity. Default per heuristics.
assumption_ledger.what_breaks_if_wrong: Mobile users would have a degraded but not broken experience.

assumption_ledger.assumption: Live / responsive preview as the user types is the expected interaction (vs. a click-to-render button).
assumption_ledger.rationale: Modern equation tools (Overleaf preview, KaTeX demos, etc.) set this expectation; tight feedback loops are core to LaTeX authoring.
assumption_ledger.what_breaks_if_wrong: Users would still get correct output, just a clunkier feel; reversible later.

assumption_ledger.assumption: Scope is math-mode LaTeX (equations), not full document compilation.
assumption_ledger.rationale: Prompt says 'mathematical equations', explicitly narrowing to math, not full \documentclass papers.
assumption_ledger.what_breaks_if_wrong: Tool would over-deliver; users expecting full LaTeX docs would be disappointed but the named goal still ships.

assumption_ledger.assumption: Errors in LaTeX input should be surfaced to the user (not silently swallowed).
assumption_ledger.rationale: Authoring tools require feedback on syntax errors; otherwise debugging is impossible.
assumption_ledger.what_breaks_if_wrong: Users would be confused by blank/broken output with no hint why.

assumption_ledger.assumption: Tool runs locally in the browser with no server-side rendering required.
assumption_ledger.rationale: LaTeX-to-HTML/SVG math rendering is a solved client-side problem; backend default-not-present.
assumption_ledger.what_breaks_if_wrong: If full TeX engine fidelity (e.g., obscure packages) were needed, would require server-side TeX.

inflection_points.topic: Input model: single equation field vs. multi-equation editor
choices: Single textarea + single rendered output (one equation at a time)
Document-style editor where multiple equations can be authored and rendered in a list
inflection_points.why_inflection: Cascades into UI layout, state model, and whether 'export' means one image or a document. Reversibility is moderate; the smaller-scope branch is the named goal floor.

choices: Export rendered output (image / SVG / MathML / copy-to-clipboard)
inflection_points.why_inflection: UX surface is real (export buttons visible or not) and option asymmetry exists (export adds format choices, file handling). The prompt says 'render' not 'export', so render-only matches the literal floor.

choices: Include a symbol/template palette to assist non-fluent LaTeX users
inflection_points.why_inflection: UX surface differs noticeably but reversible; helpers can be layered on later without disrupting core.

out_of_scope: Server-side persistence or saved equation libraries
Full LaTeX document compilation (\documentclass, sections, bibliographies)
Custom LaTeX package installation / arbitrary TeX engine fidelity
Conversion from images or handwriting to LaTeX (OCR)
